description: Field value format rules for creating and updating records via bulk_record_actions. Read schema://record-type/{recordTypeId} resource for per-field specifics.

text.notes: Primary field (first field) is required on create.

long-text / long-text-html.notes: Both content and contentHTML should be provided. contentHTML supports basic HTML tags.

number / percentage / currency.notes: Percentage is stored as decimal (0.75 = 75%). Precision and sign constraints apply per field config.

date.notes: Date-only fields: use T00:00:00.000Z. DateTime fields: include actual time. Always include timezone Z.

single-select.notes: Use the option 'name' (internal identifier), NOT 'displayName'. Read schema://record-type/{recordTypeId} resource to see available option names.

multi-select.notes: Same naming rule as single-select. Always an array, even for one value.

reference.notes: ALWAYS an array, even for single-reference fields. Record IDs start with 'Rc' + 24 hex chars. To find which record type a reference field links to, read schema://record-type/{recordTypeId} resource—it shows the linked record type ID for each reference field.

user.notes: ALWAYS an array. Use search_users to find user IDs first.

formula / lookup / created-by / created-.notes: These fields cannot be set in create/update operations. Values are computed by the server.

generalRules: Read the schema://record-type/{recordTypeId} resource to get exact field IDs and allowed values.
Field values are keyed by field ID (e.g., 'F698c581cedd4ad08868b28e6'), not by display name.
Omitting a field in an update leaves it unchanged. Setting it to null clears the value.
Bulk operations are NOT atomic — check the hasErrors flag in the response.
